scenes.narration: Model Audit. Are you using the right LLM for each job?

props.heading: /model-audit
props.subheading: Are you using the right LLM for each job?

scenes.narration: Run slash model-audit from Claude Code. You get a menu with four options. Tier check validates weight class. Task fit matches models to jobs. Cost and accuracy reviews token spend. And check docs verifies every model ID against live provider documentation.

The scan walks your codebase for every LLM integration. Results land in a verdict table with file, model, provider, and status. Here it caught a stale anthropic entry with the exact file, line number, and the suggested fix.

Action item two. Task fit. Model audit checks whether the right weight class is handling each job. It flags cheap models doing complex reasoning, and expensive models doing trivial classification. Wrong model equals wasted budget or degraded quality. Every mismatch comes with a swap recommendation.

Action item three. Cost and accuracy. This sweep cross-references your actual token spend against what the job needs. It catches overpaying, prompt inefficiency, and accuracy regressions. Every finding comes with a savings estimate so you can prioritize the fixes that move the needle first.
Stop guessing which models you are running. Run slash model-audit and know in two minutes.
